summary: Investigated hanging issues in the podcast-scraper-daily cron job. Found six root causes: (1) feedparser.parse() had no timeout — fixed by setting a socket-level default timeout of 30s around the call; (2) fetch_transcript() could stream-hang on large files — fixed by switching to chunked streaming with a 10 MB body cap; (3) download_audio() used a single scalar timeout that only guarded the connect phase — fixed with a (connect, read) tuple timeout and a 500 MB size cap, plus atomic tmp-file writes to avoid leaving partial cache files; (4) scrape-podcasts.sh ran the scrape subprocess with no wall-clock limit — fixed by wrapping in `timeout $PODCAST_SCRAPE_TIMEOUT` (default 30 min) with exit-124 detection; (5) export / export-json subprocesses in all three shell scripts had no timeouts — fixed with 120 s guards; (6) Ollama wrapper calls in audio-followup.sh and server-pipeline.sh had no limit — fixed with OLLAMA_TIMEOUT_SECS (default 300 s). All fixes committed to branch openclaw/investigate-and-fix-the-hanging-issue-in-the-podca and PR #4 opened at https://github.com/lhaggarty/podcast-scraper/pull/4.
current_status: PR #4 open and ready for review. All hanging vectors addressed with explicit timeouts. Pipeline continues to degrade gracefully on timeout (logs exit-124, falls back where possible).
